what_it_does: Acceptance tests for the ProjectionRegistry in runtime/projections, verifying that projection lenses are discovered from the projections/ directory, can be added and removed dynamically, fail loudly on malformed entries, expose correct consumer views (model, code, embeddable, records), and stay reflected in projections/AGENTS.md.
observations: Mirrors the RoleRegistry/SkillRegistry file-discovery pattern for projections.
Dynamically writes and deletes a real projections/acc_tmp_lens.py file to prove drop-in registration and un-registration.
Uses a global PASS counter incremented by a local check() helper rather than a test framework.
Includes a battery of fail-loud validation cases via a local raises() helper.
Checks that model_projections() excludes the code-produced 'lineage' lens.
Verifies embeddable() contains the embeds:true seeds and that code_projections() admits only produced_by:code lenses.
Checks every discovered projection id appears back-ticked in projections/AGENTS.md.
References a non-entry skip for leading-underscore or non-PROJECTION files.
standouts: The live filesystem drop-in/removal test is treated as the adversary-verified BAR for 'registry-is-truth'.
Fail-loud validation is exercised directly through _build_projection rather than relying on discovery alone.
Drift-home check ties the discovered registry to projections/AGENTS.md.
conventions: Acceptance test file named *_acceptance.py with a module-level docstring stating the laws being proven.
Uses discover()/rediscover() API on a registry class.
Uses plain assert and helper functions instead of pytest.
Imports implementation internals (ProjectionRegistry, Projection, _build_projection, PROJECTION_FIELDS, REQUIRED_FIELDS) for white-box validation.
concerns: The test writes and removes a file directly in the production projections/ directory, which is a side-effect on the codebase under test.
The pycache cleanup is started but not executed (pyc variable assigned, then unused).
Global mutable PASS counter and helper functions make the test order-dependent and harder to reuse.
The rediscover() method is chained after discover() with unclear semantics from this file alone.
notes: A filesystem-level acceptance suite that treats projection lenses as file-discovered registry entries and guards them with dynamic add/remove, validation, and documentation-drift checks.
questions: What are the exact contents of PROJECTION_FIELDS and REQUIRED_FIELDS?
Does rediscover() fully reset state or layer on top of discover()?
Is the pycache cleanup intentionally omitted or incomplete?
Is projections/AGENTS.md hand-maintained or generated from the registry?
What do RoleRegistry and SkillRegistry look like, and how closely does ProjectionRegistry actually mirror them?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is an acceptance test proving the stated registry-is-truth, fail-loud, and drift-home properties.
intention_signals: To establish projection lenses as a first-class, file-discovered substrate that authors can extend by dropping files, while using acceptance tests to enforce validation, consumer semantics, and documentation parity.
novelty: The 'BAR' framing and treating projection lenses as a registry with a produced_by split (model vs code), plus the explicit adversary-verified drop-in/removal proof.
contribution: A deterministic acceptance test that documents and enforces the ProjectionRegistry contract: file discovery, dynamic registration, strict validation, consumer projections, and AGENTS.md drift.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test suite for a file-discovered projection-lens registry that verifies discovery, dynamic add/remove, fail-loud validation, model/code/embeddable consumer views, and drift to AGENTS.md.
intention_for_embedding: To prove by mechanism that projection lenses are a file-discovered, authorable registry whose truth lives in the projections/ directory and remains in sync with AGENTS.md.

symbols.does: Local assertion helper that increments a global PASS counter.
symbols.description: The check(label, cond) function asserts the supplied condition, increments the module-level PASS counter on success, and prints an 'ok' line; it is the primitive used to record every acceptance assertion in this suite.

symbols.does: Local helper that expects a callable to raise ValueError or TypeError.
symbols.description: The raises(label, fn) function invokes fn() and treats a raised ValueError or TypeError as a passing check; it is used to verify that malformed projection declarations fail loudly during _build_projection.
